definition.description: Absence of one or both testes from the scrotum
definition.epidemiology: Most common genital abnormality in male neonates, especially in preterm infants

definitions.undescended_testis: Testis not descended into scrotum by birth
definitions.retractile_testis: Testis can be brought into scrotum but retracts back
definitions.ectopic_testis: Testis located outside normal path (perineum, femoral, suprapubic)

pathophysiology: Failure of testicular descent through inguinal canal
May be due to hormonal, mechanical, or anatomical causes

history: History of inguinal hernia or scrotal swelling
examination: Examine in warm room with calm infant
Palpate from iliac crest along inguinal canal
Determine location: scrotal, inguinal, abdominal, ectopic
Differentiate retractile vs true undescended testis

types.location: Inguinal canal or just outside scrotum

general_principles: Most testes descend spontaneously by 6 months
No role of routine hormonal therapy (hCG)

parent_counseling: Condition is common in newborn males — नवजात लड़कों में यह आम समस्या है
Most cases resolve by 6 months — अधिकतर 6 महीने तक अपने आप ठीक हो जाती है
Surgery is simple and safe if required — ज़रूरत हो तो ऑपरेशन सरल और सुरक्षित है
Early surgery improves fertility — जल्दी ऑपरेशन से प्रजनन क्षमता बेहतर रहती है
Cancer risk is low but higher than normal — कैंसर का खतरा कम है पर सामान्य से ज़्यादा
Regular follow-up till puberty is essential — यौवन तक नियमित जांच ज़रूरी है
flowchart_logic: If both testes in scrotum → Normal
If palpable in canal → Observe till 6 months
warning_signs: Bilateral non-palpable testes — दोनों तरफ़ अंडकोष न मिलना
Empty scrotum with ambiguous genitalia — खाली अंडकोष थैली और अस्पष्ट जननांग
Associated inguinal hernia — साथ में हर्निया होना
Ascending testis (previously normal) — पहले सामान्य था पर अब ऊपर चला गया
Testis not descended by 6 months — 6 महीने तक अंडकोष नीचे न आना
expected_course: Spontaneous descent occurs in most cases by 6 months of age, especially in term infants. If testis has not descended by 6 months, it is unlikely to descend spontaneously. Orchidopexy is recommended between 6-18 months for best fertility and malignancy outcomes. Retractile testis needs annual monitoring as it may become ascending.
key_clinical_points: Examine in warm room with calm, relaxed infant for accurate assessment
Two-handed technique: one hand from iliac crest along canal, other hand palpates scrotum
Differentiate retractile (can be brought down and stays) from true UDT (springs back)
Bilateral non-palpable testes require urgent karyotype and hormonal workup to exclude DSD
No role of routine hormonal therapy (hCG) — surgery is the definitive treatment
Orchidopexy by 18 months optimizes fertility and reduces malignancy risk

severity_assessment.mild: Unilateral palpable UDT in inguinal canal, term infant <6 months — observe, follow-up at 6 months
severity_assessment.moderate: Persistent unilateral UDT at 6 months, palpable — refer for orchidopexy (6-18 months)
severity_assessment.severe: Bilateral non-palpable testes or UDT with ambiguous genitalia — urgent pediatric surgery and endocrine evaluation

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Every 3 months until surgery or descent
